description: Failed token exchange at Token Vault

auth0_client.description: The client or SDK used to do this request, if any. This is based on the `Auth0-Client` HTTP header.

client_id.description: Client related to the API call. Clients are also called applications. The underlying application may not be owned by your tenant and may not be accessible. For failure logs, this may be a valid ID, an invalid one, or any ID.

client_name.description: Client name associated with the client_id, when available. May be empty string.

connection.description: Name of the connection, when available. The connection here matches the `connection_id` field.

connection_id.description: ID of the connection on which the auth flow is happening or connection of the user doing the action, when applicable. For failure logs this field may contain an invalid ID.

date.description: The date when the event occurred in ISO 8601 format

description.description: Description of the event. This can also contain a description of the issue for failure logs.

audit_context.description: Value of the `audit_context` claim echoed from the subject token JWT.

enterprise_connection.description: Name of the connection, when available. The connection here matches the `connection_id` field.

enterprise_connection_id.description: ID of the connection on which the auth flow is happening or connection of the user doing the action, when applicable. For failure logs this field may contain an invalid ID.

failure_reason.description: Reason the token exchange failed. Emits `unknown` for unclassified failure modes.

is_privileged_worker.description: True when the token exchange is performed via the Privileged Worker flow.

environment_name.description: The name of the environment where the event occurred

identity_user_id.description: ID for the user as reported by the IdP.

ip.description: The IP address associated with the request. For an auth related log this should be the end user IP. For a machine-to-machine auth flow this should be the IP of the computer doing the authentication. For management logs like `sapi` and `mgmt_api_read` this should be the IP of the admin using the manage dashboard.

is_mobile.description: Whether the request originated from a mobile device. Only present when user_agent is available.

organization_id.description: ID of the organization related to this auth flow or action, when applicable. For failure logs, the value may be a valid ID, and invalid ID, or any other string.

organization_name.description: Name of the organization in `organization_id`, when applicable and valid. May be an empty string.

strategy.description: Strategy of the connection in `connection_id`, when applicable and valid. This may be empty string.

strategy_type.description: Category of the strategy in `strategy`, when applicable. This may be empty string.

type.description: Failed Exchange of Refresh Token for Token for 3rd party API

user_agent.description: The user_agent behind this log, when available

user_id.description: ID of the user behind the auth flow, or of the user executing the action, whichever applies. For machine to machine auth flows, this field may contain an empty string. For failure logs, the value may be a valid ID, an invalid ID, or empty string

user_name.description: Username of the user related to the ID is shown in `user_id`, when available. For failure logs, the value may be a valid username, an invalid username, or empty string.
